title: Constipation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Defines the exact Dubai telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04

red_flags.text: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped bowel frequency, stool character, straining, incomplete evacuation, bleeding, abdominal pain, and medication context.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Specifies nature, onset, duration, bowel diary, secondary causes, straining, incomplete evacuation, abdominal pain, bleeding, and medication context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04

red_flags.text: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped bleeding, weight-loss, and family-history prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists new onset in older people, anaemia, rectal bleeding, family history, tenesmus, weight loss, and persistent unresponsive constipation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04

red_flags.text: Lists emergency bleeding and specialist referral criteria for constipation.
red_flags.rationale: Reviewed to verify escalation criteria; no autonomous referral instruction was mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists emergency bleeding and specialist referral criteria for constipation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Constipation – 04

investigations.text: Limits laboratory and colorectal imaging evaluation to persistent, unresponsive, or suspected secondary disease.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped blood and imaging documentation only in the stated persistent or suspected-disease context.
investigations.evidence_extract: Limits laboratory and colorectal imaging evaluation to persistent, unresponsive, or suspected secondary disease.

source_limitations: The baseline workflow is setting-agnostic and broader than the exact source.
The IBS diagnosis label, obstruction chip, and treatment choices remain unsupported by the exact mapped sections.

All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
